outcome_text: Pre-trained models which are used for development are monitored as part of AI system regular monitoring and maintenance.
scoring_text: Knowledge of pre-trained model monitoring requirements, including integrating pre-trained model monitoring into AI system maintenance workflows, tracking model drift and performance degradation, and assessing pre-trained model risks as part of regular AI system monitoring activities.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of pre-trained model monitoring requirements, including integrating pre-trained model monitoring into AI system maintenance workflows, tracking model drift and performance degradation, and assessing pre-trained model risks as part of regular AI system monitoring activities.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift detection, performance monitoring, retraining triggers, and decommissioning criteria.
comments: NIST AI RMF MG-3.2 (MANAGE) equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.677, cosine: 0.734). Secondary: AI-K-024 (AI, cosine: 0.703), AI-K-007 (AI, cosine: 0.615).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.677). Shared vocabulary: [ai, drift, knowledge, model, monitoring, performance]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [activities, assessing, degradation, integrating, maintenance, part] vs WIDAI terms [criteria, decommissioning, detection, lifecycle, management, retraining] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.906 (P=0.898, R=0.913). Balanced P/R — the 35-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.734 > STS 0.677 (gap +0.057). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, drift, knowledge, model, monitoring] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 27.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, drift, knowledge, model, monitoring, performance]. NIST AI RMF-only: [activities, assessing, degradation, integrating, maintenance, part]. WIDAI-only: [criteria, decommissioning, detection, lifecycle, management, retraining].
